summaryText: Guest Speaker James Braxton Powell opened with a call to gratitude and praise, emphasizing that God meets us in our seasons of pain and struggle. Using the story of the widow in 2 Kings 4, he illustrated how God provides when we are obedient with the 'little' we have. The widow, facing the loss of her husband and the threat of her sons being taken as slaves, was instructed by Elisha to gather empty jars and pour out her only valuable possession: a small flask of oil. As she obeyed, the oil miraculously multiplied, filling every jar, providing enough to pay her debts and live on the surplus. Pastor James connected this to the idea that our 'oil' represents the gifts, callings, and resources God has placed within us. He challenged the congregation to stop overlooking their God-given 'oil,' to see the 'empty jars'—difficult circumstances or people—as divine assignments, and to pour out what they have in faith. The message concluded with an invitation to accept salvation and a call to live in the overflow of God's blessing through faithful obedience.
mainPoints: God meets us in our pain and asks us to use what little we have, trusting Him to multiply it.
Our God-given gifts and callings ('oil') are meant to be poured out to meet the needs around us, transforming empty situations.
Obedience to God's word, even in small steps, leads to provision, overflow, and a lasting legacy.

versesMentioned.text: The wife of a man from the company of the prophets cried out to Elisha, 'Your servant my husband is dead, and you know that he revered the LORD. But now his creditor is coming to take my two boys as his slaves.' Elisha replied to her, 'How can I help you? Tell me, what do you have in your house?' 'Your servant has nothing there at all,' she said, 'except a small jar of olive oil.' Elisha said, 'Go around and ask all your neighbors for empty jars. Don’t ask for just a few. Then go inside and shut the door behind you and your sons. Pour oil into all the jars, and as each is filled, put it to one side.' She left him and shut the door behind her and her sons. They brought the jars to her and she kept pouring. When all the jars were full, she said to her son, 'Bring me another one.' But he replied, 'There is not a jar left.' Then the oil stopped flowing. She went and told the man of God, and he said, 'Go, sell the oil and pay your debts. You and your sons can live on what is left.'
Not only so, but we also glory in our sufferings, because we know that suffering produces perseverance; perseverance, character; and character, hope.
But we have this treasure in jars of clay to show that this all-surpassing power is from God and not from us.

Monday.scripture: 2 Kings 4:2 - 'Tell me, what do you have in your house?'
Monday.action: Take 5 minutes to write down one gift, resource, or passion God has given you.
Monday.prayer: God, open my eyes to see what You have already placed in my hands. Help me to value it.

Tuesday.scripture: 2 Kings 4:3 - 'Go around and ask all your neighbors for empty jars.'
Tuesday.action: Identify one 'empty jar' in your life—a challenging person or circumstance—and pray for them specifically.
Tuesday.prayer: Lord, shift my perspective. Help me see the needs around me not as burdens, but as opportunities for You to work.

Wednesday.scripture: 2 Kings 4:4 - 'Then go inside and shut the door behind you and your sons. Pour oil into all the jars...'
Wednesday.action: In a private moment, take one step of obedience with your 'oil.' Share an encouraging word, offer help, or start a project you've been putting off.
Wednesday.prayer: Father, I choose to trust and obey You today, even in a small step. Multiply my faithful action for Your glory.

Thursday.scripture: Romans 5:3-4 - '...suffering produces perseverance; perseverance, character; and character, hope.'
Thursday.action: When you face a difficulty today, pause for 60 seconds and thank God that He is at work, building your character and hope.
Thursday.prayer: God, I praise You in this situation, trusting that You are producing something good in me. My hope is in You.

Friday.focus: Pour out until the need is met.
Friday.action: Look for a practical way to 'pour out' for someone today—offer your time, a listening ear, or a tangible gift.
Friday.prayer: Holy Spirit, guide me to someone in need. Use me to be a conduit of Your provision and love today.

Saturday.scripture: 2 Kings 4:7 - 'Go, sell the oil and pay your debts. You and your sons can live on what is left.'
Saturday.action: Reflect on one way God has provided for you this week. Share that story of blessing with a friend or family member.
Saturday.prayer: Jehovah Jireh, thank You for being my provider. I trust that my obedience leads to Your abundance. Let my life testify to Your overflow.
